primary_modes.rationale: The task centres on a UI component library specification (obers_ui v4), including a Theming System and Naming & Conventions that directly govern user-facing component design and behaviour.

Naming & Conventions and Package Structure are canonical DX concerns: they define how developers discover, import, and consume the library. Getting these right has outsized impact on developer ergonomics and adoption.

secondary_modes.rationale: The work is scoped as discrete, tracked requirement atoms (REQ-0001 through REQ-0003) with an explicit Definition of Done, format/analyze/test gates, and a coverage ledger — all hallmarks of feature delivery work.

generic.rationale: Anchors why obers_ui v4 needs a new specification; prevents scope creep across all four parts.
Defines success criteria for Part 1: what 'fully implemented' means for naming, package structure, and theming.
Explicit exclusions prevent premature work on Parts 2-4 or unrelated library features during this sprint.
Stack context is not provided; assumptions about framework (React/Vue/Svelte), build tooling, and package manager must be stated to unblock decisions.
Environment and timeline are unknown; constraints (semver compat, peer-dep ranges, bundle size limits) should be surfaced before design solidifies.
Already partially specified in the task (format, analyze, test, coverage ledger); needs to be formalised and version-controlled.
Each REQ atom needs machine-verifiable acceptance criteria so CI can gate the coverage ledger update automatically.
All project checks (format, analyze, test) must pass; test cases for naming rules and theme token resolution need to be codified.
Medium risk tolerance and missing stack/environment context mean open questions should be tracked explicitly rather than deferred.
Low urgency for Part 1 since it is spec-only work, but rollback strategy for theme breaking changes becomes relevant in later parts.

specialized.rationale: Naming & Conventions (REQ-0001) and Package Structure (REQ-0002) are entirely about developer ergonomics; a dedicated ergonomics artefact ensures consistency across the full library.
Package Structure (REQ-0002) defines the public API surface of the library — entry points, barrel exports, tree-shaking boundaries. This is SDK design work.
If obers_ui ships a CLI (init, codegen, theme-builder), the interface should be specified now to align with naming conventions while they are still fluid.
Theming System (REQ-0003) benefits from visual token maps / design-token diagrams to align designers and engineers on the expected output before code is written.

recommended_specialist_agents.rationale: Owns the overall library architecture — component API surface, tree-shaking, SSR compatibility, and how the theming system integrates with the package structure.
Directly responsible for REQ-0001 (naming conventions) and REQ-0002 (package structure); defines import patterns, barrel exports, and tooling config (eslint rules, prettier config).
Provides evidence-based input on theming patterns (REQ-0003) — design token taxonomy, semantic vs. primitive token separation, accessibility colour contrast requirements.
The specification is v4 of a complete library doc; a technical writer ensures naming conventions and theming guidance are unambiguous, consistent, and consumable by external contributors.
Definition of Done explicitly requires all checks (format, analyze, test) to pass and the coverage ledger to be updated; QA engineers own the test scaffolding and gate automation.

assumptions: obers_ui targets a JavaScript/TypeScript ecosystem (most likely React, but framework is unconfirmed).
The library is published to npm and follows standard semver; breaking changes require a major version bump.
The 'coverage ledger' is a custom tracking document (or CI artefact) that maps requirement atoms to implementation and test coverage — not standard code coverage alone.
Parts 2-4 of the specification exist but are out of scope for this task.
The project already has a working CI pipeline that runs format, analyze (lint/typecheck), and test commands.
Medium risk tolerance means breaking changes to naming or theming are acceptable in this pre-release version but must be communicated clearly in changelogs.
No accessibility (a11y) or internationalisation (i18n) requirements are imposed by REQ-0001 through REQ-0003, though REQ-0003 (theming) should account for contrast ratios.

open_questions.question: Which UI framework(s) does obers_ui target (React, Vue, Svelte, framework-agnostic Web Components)?
open_questions.why_it_matters: This determines component naming conventions (PascalCase JSX vs. kebab-case custom elements), package entry points, and theming system implementation (CSS custom properties vs. CSS-in-JS vs. design tokens).
open_questions.suggested_default_if_unknown: Assume React with TypeScript and CSS custom properties for theming until confirmed.

open_questions.question: What is the intended public API scope of REQ-0002 (Package Structure)? Monorepo with sub-packages, single package with barrel exports, or both?
open_questions.why_it_matters: Drives tree-shaking architecture, versioning strategy, and how consumers import individual components vs. the full library.
open_questions.suggested_default_if_unknown: Assume single npm package with per-component deep-import paths (e.g., obers-ui/button) and a full-library barrel export.

open_questions.question: What is the theming system's implementation strategy (REQ-0003): design tokens (Style Dictionary), CSS custom properties, a JS theme object, or a combination?
open_questions.why_it_matters: This is the most architecturally consequential decision in Part 1 — it affects runtime performance, SSR support, and how third-party tools (Figma, Storybook) integrate.
open_questions.suggested_default_if_unknown: Assume CSS custom properties as the runtime layer, with a JSON design token source-of-truth compiled via Style Dictionary.

open_questions.question: Does the 'coverage ledger' have an existing format/schema, or does it need to be designed as part of this task?
open_questions.why_it_matters: If the ledger schema is undefined, designing it adds scope to Part 1 and may block CI gate implementation for all four parts.
open_questions.suggested_default_if_unknown: Assume the ledger is a YAML file mapping REQ-IDs to { status, test_file, coverage_pct } and is committed alongside source code.

open_questions.question: Are there existing v1-v3 naming conventions or a legacy API that v4 must remain backward-compatible with?
open_questions.why_it_matters: If v4 is a clean break, naming can be redesigned freely. If migration support is required, REQ-0001 must include deprecation/alias rules, adding significant scope.
open_questions.suggested_default_if_unknown: Assume v4 is a greenfield redesign with no backward-compatibility requirement (breaking change, new major version).

pmSpecV1: Let me check the current state of the key modified files to understand what's already done vs. what needs to be planned.Let me check the current state of a few more key files to understand what's already implemented vs. what needs planning.
architectDesignV1: Let me check the current state of the key modified files to understand what's already done vs. what needs to be planned.Let me check the current state of a few more key files to understand what's already implemented vs. what needs planning.
implementationNotes: REQ-0001 (Naming & Conventions): naming_conventions_test.dart already exists and passes — all lib/src files correctly use adjective-style boolean names (enabled, loading, etc.) with no is/has prefixes. REQ-0002 (Package Structure): lib/obers_ui.dart barrel export file is complete with all components organized by category. REQ-0003 (Theming System): OiThemeData, OiDynamicTheme, and OiPlayground are fully implemented and tested. Actions taken: (1) ran dart format to bring all 330 files into compliance, (2) fixed unused_local_variable warning in oi_list_view_test.dart by using the searchedQuery value in an assertion. All 2443 tests pass, no warnings from flutter analyze.
